creator: Alexander Terp
description:
The `in` operator has no handler for checking if a map exists as an element of a list. This errors instead of working.

**Repro:**
```
items = [{"a": 1}, {"b": 2}]
print({"a": 1} in items)
// Error: "Invalid operand types: cannot do 'map in list'"
```

**Root cause:** In `core/expr_ops.go`, the operand type switch has no top-level `case *RadMap:` handler, so there is no code path for map-in-list checks.

**Test files:** `bugs/collections/58_map_in_list_comparison.rad`, `bugs/operators/110d_in_supported_types.rad`
title: map in list not supported by in operator
